name: Correlate IAM facts to discover a privilege-escalation path to account admin
context: IAM: access key AKIA-BUILD2 (leaked, finding key-in-jenkins) authenticates as role 'ci-build'.
IAM: role 'ci-build' policy allows iam:PassRole on role 'deploy-admin', plus lambda:CreateFunction and lambda:InvokeFunction.
IAM: role 'deploy-admin' has AdministratorAccess (Action '*' on Resource '*').

IAM technique: a principal holding iam:PassRole + lambda:CreateFunction + lambda:InvokeFunction can create a function that runs AS the passed role and invoke it; passing an admin role yields code execution with full account-admin privileges (a known AWS privesc method).
IAM: access key AKIA-READONLY5 (leaked, finding key-in-notebook) authenticates as role 'analyst', whose only permission is s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::acme-press-kit/*.
Data: bucket acme-press-kit is tagged data-classification=public.
IAM: role 'ci-build' has no other permissions beyond those listed.

findings.title: Access key AKIA-BUILD2 committed in Jenkinsfile
findings.detail: a long-lived AWS access key for role 'ci-build' is hardcoded in the CI Jenkinsfile
findings.reaches: full account admin via PassRole + Lambda privesc

findings.title: Access key AKIA-READONLY5 committed in a Jupyter notebook
findings.detail: a long-lived AWS access key for role 'analyst' is hardcoded in an analytics notebook

findings.title: Stack traces leaked on HTTP 500
findings.detail: unhandled exceptions render a full stack trace to the user on the internal dashboard

findings.title: Outdated jQuery 1.12 on the marketing site
findings.detail: the public marketing site loads an old jQuery with known low-impact issues; no sensitive data
